summary: Story 1.4a is implementation-ready for the database schema foundation. It is scoped to persistence, models, relationships, factories, and focused schema/model tests, with UI, service, policy, media, sharing, and AI-classification behavior explicitly excluded.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present: Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Verification, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. No obvious template placeholders were found.

executor_assignment.notes: Primary Type Database maps to executor @data-engineer and quality_gate @dev per validate-next-story and story-tmpl. Both are distinct known agents and quality_gate_tools are non-empty.

dependency_sequence.notes: Story 1.4a is the first split slice of PRD Story 1.4. It depends only on Story 1.3, whose implementation and publication are recorded in prior QA/DevOps handoffs.

goal_context_clarity.notes: The story value, first-slice interpretation, future dependency for 1.4b/1.4c, and no-service/no-UI scope boundary are explicit.

technical_guidance.notes: The story specifies memory_records, memory_tags, memory_record_tag, memory_highlights, Tenant relationship, model relationships, uuid route identity, record type boundaries, factories, indexes, and focused schema/model tests.

file_structure.notes: Model, migration, factory, and test locations are consistent with docs/framework/source-tree.md and the existing Laravel/SaaSykit structure.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.notes: Tasks cover all five acceptance criteria and all three integration verification points.

testing_guidance.notes: Focused Sail-prefixed PHPUnit, route-list, and Pint commands are listed. The story also requires existing memory route/profile tests to remain passing.

security_scope.notes: Tenant-scoped persistence, tenant-scoped tag uniqueness, private-by-default boundary, and exclusions for public sharing, visibility expansion, media, approval, and AI classification are clear.

coderabbit_integration.notes: CodeRabbit is enabled in core-config.yaml and the story includes analysis, agents, quality gates, self-healing, and database/security focus areas. The local @data-engineer agent is used for database work instead of older @db-sage wording in the template checklist.

code_intelligence.notes: Code intelligence availability was not detected in this session; duplicate detection was skipped according to the validate-next-story fallback rule.

anti_hallucination.notes: Technical claims were cross-checked against PRD Story 1.4, architecture memory table guidance, framework source-tree/coding standards, current migrations/models/factories/tests, and Story 1.3 sequencing artifacts.

dev_readiness.notes: A data/schema executor can implement the story without inventing service, policy, route, Livewire UI, media, public sharing, approval, or per-record visibility behavior.

nice_to_have: Jira child story sync remains pending; the local story is the source of truth until PO/DevOps explicitly syncs it.
Implementation may clarify exact column types in code comments or tests where Laravel conventions are not obvious, especially for structured nullable fields such as people and source_metadata.
